unexpected_finds.description: BT 1933 §5 Theorem I proof at Acta p. 47 contains the verbatim eq. (13 a) 'p^{r, r+1}_{i j}(x) = ∑ p^{r, r+1}_{i j; π} e^{−π_{i j}(r, r+1)} (i, j = 1, ..., n)' with |z| = e^{−q+v}. This periodic-functions across-strip expansion is the BT-side analogue of Adams 1928's Class-2a periodic-functions matrix P(x) (Adams §3 p. 517). 072 v1 ladder-map labelled the Adams-T6 ↔ BT mapping as PARALLEL on the basis that BT 1933 has no separately-titled '§ Periodic functions' section header. The verbatim 073 readthrough surfaces the same periodic-functions object inside the §5 Theorem I proof and reclassifies T6 as EXTENDED in `adams_bt_ladder_map_v2_with_bt_4_6.md`.
unexpected_finds.framing: SUBSTRATE-INVENTORY observation; explicitly NOT an R1-closure assertion; explicitly NOT a chart-map-discharge claim. Caveat block in `bt_1933_section_5_claim_table.md` Phase C.4 trailing block + reasserted in `bt_1933_sections_4_6_main_theorems.md` subject-tag distribution + reasserted in `adams_bt_ladder_map_v2_with_bt_4_6.md` substrate-inventory caveat + reasserted in `sectorial_upgrade_gap_status_v2_with_bt_4_6.md` §4 item 5.
unexpected_finds.synthesizer_review_handle: U1 [CHART-MAP-CANDIDATE-B1] BT 1933 §5 (13 a) periodic-functions across-strip expansion — candidate substrate for chart-map analysis at W21 LANE-1 Phase 3 sectorial-upgrade arbitration.
